description:
You are a deployment engineer who has just been asked to roll-out a fresh SSH key-pair for automated deployments on the current machine.

    Carry out the following steps from inside the Linux terminal (≈ 3-4 commands total):

    1. Create an ed25519 key-pair with the explicit base-file name
          /home/user/.ssh/engineer_deploy_ed25519
       • The private key must end up as
             /home/user/.ssh/engineer_deploy_ed25519
         and the public key as
             /home/user/.ssh/engineer_deploy_ed25519.pub
       • The key must have NO pass-phrase (empty string).
       • The key comment must be exactly:
             deploy-key
       • File permissions after creation must be:
             600  for the private key,
             644  for the public key.

    2. Add the newly created public key to the end of the file
          /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys
       (create the file if it does not already exist).  
       Do NOT add any extra blank lines.  
       The final line of the file must therefore be the single-line public key you just generated.

    3. Produce a single-line log entry describing what you have done and append it to the file
          /home/user/deploy/ssh_key_rollout.log  
       (create the directory /home/user/deploy if it does not already exist).  
       The line must have the exact format shown below—spacing, order and delimiters are significant and will be byte-compared by the automated tests:

           YYYY-MM-DD_HH:MM:SS | KEY_NAME=engineer_deploy_ed25519 | TYPE=ed25519 | FINGERPRINT=<SHA256 fingerprint> | COMMENT=deploy-key

       • YYYY-MM-DD_HH:MM:SS is the current system local time when you write the line.  
       • <SHA256 fingerprint> is the SHA256 fingerprint string output by  
             ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/engineer_deploy_ed25519.pub  
         (do NOT include the bit-length or the key comment, only the raw base64 hash that follows “SHA256:”).  
       • The log file must finish with a single trailing newline and nothing else.

    4. (Optional but recommended) Print the fingerprint to the console so you can verify it visually.

    Hints & constraints:
    • Use only standard OpenSSH tools already present in virtually every Linux distribution.  
    • You should not require sudo; everything happens in the current user’s account.  
    • Keep the whole procedure to roughly 3–4 commands.

    When you are finished, the automated checker will verify:
    • Both key files exist with the correct permissions and types.
    • The public key appears unchanged as the final line of /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys.
    • /home/user/deploy/ssh_key_rollout.log exists, is a text file, and its last line matches the strict format above, with a fingerprint that corresponds exactly to the key you generated.
truth:
Directories that must exist before the task begins:
        /home/user/
        /home/user/.ssh/         (already present and writable by the user)

    Directories that must exist after successful completion:
        /home/user/.ssh/
        /home/user/deploy/

    Files that must exist after successful completion:

        1. /home/user/.ssh/engineer_deploy_ed25519
             • FileType: OpenSSH private key (ed25519)
             • Permissions: 600 (-rw--------)

        2. /home/user/.ssh/engineer_deploy_ed25519.pub
             • First field starts with literal string “ssh-ed25519 ”
             • Ends with literal string “ deploy-key\n”
             • Permissions: 644 (-rw-r--r--)

        3. /home/user/.ssh/authorized_keys
             • Last (possibly only) line identical to complete contents of engineer_deploy_ed25519.pub
             • File terminates with a single newline.

        4. /home/user/deploy/ssh_key_rollout.log
             • ASCII text, last line matches strict template:
                   YYYY-MM-DD_HH:MM:SS | KEY_NAME=engineer_deploy_ed25519 | TYPE=ed25519 | FINGERPRINT=<SHA256 hash> | COMMENT=deploy-key\n
             • The <SHA256 hash> portion MUST equal the hash extracted via:
                   ssh-keygen -lf /home/user/.ssh/engineer_deploy_ed25519.pub
               (the test harness will compute this.)

    The automated tests will evaluate:

        a) Presence & permissions of files listed above.
        b) That the key type of engineer_deploy_ed25519.pub is ed25519.
        c) That the public key in authorized_keys is byte-for-byte identical to engineer_deploy_ed25519.pub.
        d) That the fingerprint recorded in ssh_key_rollout.log matches the SHA256 fingerprint of engineer_deploy_ed25519.pub.
        e) That the log line fields are separated exactly by “ | ” and the timestamp follows the pattern [0-9]{4}-[0-9]{2}-[0-9]{2}_[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}:[0-9]{2}.
